attacking_style: Attacks from established breakaways in the final kilometer, preferring gradients around 7% where tactical patience allows a well-timed late move rather than long-range efforts.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support, relying on breakaway opportunities where individual timing and positioning matter more than domestique assistance.
terrain_preference: Medium-gradient climbs (7%) in final kilometers suit best; capable of extended resistance in solo breakaways but most dangerous in small groups with sprint finish on gradient.
vulnerability: Lacks firepower for sustained climbing against elite GC riders; relies on breakaway success rather than competing when favorites engage, suggesting limited recovery from prolonged efforts.
key_patterns: Deploys tactical patience in breakaways, waiting until final 600m before committing attack (2 observations)
Breakaway specialist who extends efforts against peloton chase across multiple Grand Tours (2 observations)
Thrives on 7% gradient finishes where experience and timing outweigh pure power (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2020 showing 'contract-year desperation' suggests declining competitiveness from 2017 Vuelta victory performance
